summary: Script uses proper bash conventions (set -euo pipefail, quoted variables, consistent err() error handling). Workflow follows YAML best practices (pinned actions@v4, least-privilege permissions, proper secret handling, idempotent steps). Conciseness is excellent—no dead code or duplication. Readability is strong with clear naming and logical flow, though complex jq filters (lines 90-107 in script, especially the multiline check() function) lack inline explanation. Critical gap: task requires bats-core test framework and TDD methodology; workflow validates script through custom harness (expected-mode.txt + jq checks) rather than invoking `bats` tests. Missing: test fixtures (fixtures/*.json), test suite (*.bats files), and act-result.txt artifact. Maintainability suffers—no regression test coverage means future changes risk silent breakage. Code quality is solid, but deliverable is incomplete per requirements.
